description: Degradation, unreliability, breakage, and limited uses. How the item falls apart or fails when you need it most.
limitations: Fragile
Fine craftsmanship and delicate as glass. One solid hit is all it takes.
Creeping Failure
Each use winds the spring tighter. Sooner or later, and always without warning, something inside snaps.
Shifting Nature
Every third use changes something fundamental about the item. New quirks emerge like weeds, and none of them are welcome.
Limited Charges
A finite reservoir of power that drains with every use. When it is empty, it is just an object.
Slippery
It slides from pockets, rolls off tables, and slips through fingers. Keeping hold of it is a full-time occupation.
Wild Card
It does what it wants, when it wants. Sometimes the result is spectacular. Sometimes it is a disaster.
Unreliable
It works beautifully three times out of five. The other two, it does nothing at all, or something worse.
Shifting Mass
Its weight lurches unpredictably from feather-light to brutally heavy. Carrying it is a constant surprise.
Bulky
It takes up far more space than it should. Fitting it alongside anything else is a logistics problem.
Volatile Output
Some days it could level a wall. Other days, it barely sparks. There is no way to predict which.
Single Use
One moment of brilliance, then it crumbles to dust. Make the shot count because there will not be a second.
Daily Limit
It gives everything once, then goes dark until the next sunrise. Patience is not optional.
Slow Decay
Cracks spread, edges dull, and the magic frays a little more each day. It is dying, and nothing can stop it.
Diminishing
It shrinks visibly with each activation. Eventually the wielder holds nothing at all.
Crowd Shy
The more people nearby, the weaker it becomes. In a crowd, it is barely a whisper of its true self.
Fading Edge
Each use dulls it slightly. The first strike is always the best, and it is all downhill from there.
Slow Bloom
The effect arrives late. By the time it kicks in, the moment that needed it has often already passed.
Damaged Goods
Pieces are missing or cracked. It functions, but barely, and every use feels like tempting fate.
Close Quarters
Its reach is pitiful. You have to be dangerously close for it to do anything at all.
Untamed
It bucks and veers like a wild animal. Controlling it takes everything the wielder has, and sometimes that is not enough.
Long Fuse
It expires quietly after extended service. One day it simply does not respond, and no amount of coaxing brings it back.
Short Fuse
Brief and bright. It burns through its lifespan fast, leaving the wielder empty-handed sooner than expected.
Glass Jaw
Any hit to the wielder disrupts it completely. Pain and impact break whatever connection keeps it functioning.
Arcane Static
Other enchanted objects nearby cause interference. The magic crackles, stutters, and sometimes cancels entirely.
Slow Starter
It needs time to wake up. Patience is required before each use while the magic gathers itself.
Pack Hog
It demands all available carrying space. The wielder must choose between this item and everything else.
Misfire
It goes off at random. In a pack, in a pocket, in the middle of the night. It has no respect for timing.
Brief Spark
The effect blazes bright and vanishes in moments. Whatever it does, it does quickly or not at all.
Fleeting Magic
Its effects fade fast. What it gives, it takes back before long, leaving no lasting mark.
